blurb:
Cody got adopted, spent two months in a home that genuinely loved him, and came back because they couldn't keep up with him. That's honest, and honestly? It's the best information you could have going in. He's a three-year-old cattle dog mix, about 33 pounds, and he runs on full throttle. The volunteers describe him as a strong, lovable guy with a sweet nature, and he's backed that up by going through the Mostly Mutts Prison Pups training program.

He loves doggie daycare, plays well with other dogs when they're a good match for his style, and calmed right down during his shelter bath once the belly rubs started. He's going to charm you with his looks or wear you out with his energy. Probably both.

He's a little mouthy and wiggly during play, which is pretty standard for cattle dog mixes. Skip the toddlers since he's strong enough to accidentally knock a small kid over. Older kids, active adults, someone with a yard and a plan for burning energy, that's his person. Cats are unknown.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Call 770-272-6888 or head to mostlymutts.org to set up a time to meet Cody. Bring your walking shoes.
shelter_hours: Please call or visit mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Return adoption with full transparency about energy needs is a refreshingly honest pitch that builds trust with active-lifestyle readers
• Prison Pups training plus documented return reason gives editors a concrete, relatable story angle with no ambiguity
• Prior adoption and return cycle indicates he has been circling the shelter longer than a first-time resident, adding moderate urgency

blurb:
She came in from animal control, spent time in a few foster homes, and every single person who's had her says the same thing: sweetest dog ever. Glynnis is an eight-year-old terrier mix, about 41 pounds, and she's the kind of dog who melts into you for scratches and makes little dreaming noises in her sleep. The volunteers call her gentle, cuddly, and easy-going. She's not wrong to have that reputation.

She knows how to give kisses on command and will absolutely let you know when the delivery driver shows up. She's been through the Mostly Mutts Prison Pups training program, so she's got real skills and real manners. Senior dogs don't always get a fair shot at adoption, but Glynnis has plenty of good years left and a whole lot of love to give.

She does have a preference: she got along well with male dogs but wasn't a fan of the cat in her foster home. Kids ten and up should be a great fit. She's currently off-site for additional training, so you'll need to call ahead to find out when she's available to meet. Worth the extra step.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw, about twenty minutes from East Cobb. Call 770-272-6888 to ask about Glynnis and schedule a time to come say hello.
shelter_hours: Please call or visit mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Senior dog with Prison Pups training and unanimous five-star foster reviews — a rare, ready-to-love package
• Age bias against senior dogs combined with her off-site training status creates compelling urgency for newsletter readers
• Multiple foster homes, ongoing external training, and senior status all signal an extended shelter journey deserving spotlight

blurb:
Cecelia has already been through the adoption process once. She was adopted from Mostly Mutts, had a rough stretch that changed her, and came back scared and shut down. She's three years old, about 37 pounds, and a terrier-boxer mix. That return wasn't her fault. Sometimes life just doesn't work out the first time.

She's in a foster home now and doing great. Once she trusts you, she goes full lap dog, whole-body wags, the works. Her foster family says she's the perfect housemate. She's already mastered sit, stay, come, and leave it through the Mostly Mutts University training program. She just needs someone who's going to move at her pace and not rush her.

She does need to be your only dog at home. She tends to guard her person around other dogs in close quarters, so a single-dog household is the right fit. She's also better with calmer kids fifteen and up, given her history with new people. Don't grab at her or reach over her head right away. Let her come to you, and she absolutely will.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Visit mostlymutts.org to submit an inquiry about Cecelia, or call to set up a meet. She's worth being patient for.
shelter_hours: Please call or visit mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Return adoption story with a trauma-to-trust redemption arc is the most emotionally compelling narrative in the entire candidate set
• Specific placement requirements (only dog, calm teens+) narrow the pool but give readers a clear call to action if they're the right fit
• Returned once, now rebuilding in foster care — the layered history signals significant time in the system and high feature urgency

blurb:
Cannoli came in from animal control just recently, and he's still getting his bearings. The volunteers at Mostly Mutts will tell you straight up: he's a little shy right now. Not unfriendly, just new to all of this. He came in with his siblings, he feels safe around them, and he's starting to warm up to the people who are making a point to spend time with him.

He's a tuxedo kitten, about 2.5 months old and right around 2 pounds. Already litter trained, up to date on shots, microchipped, and neutered. The foundation is all there. What he needs now is a patient home where someone gives him a little room to settle in and builds some trust with him over the first few weeks. That's not a big ask for a kitten. They turn a corner fast when they feel safe.

He does well with other cats and would likely do fine with gentle kids. Dog compatibility is unknown, so if you have one, a meet and greet first would be worth doing.

Adoption fee is $175. Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Visit their website or stop by to meet Cannoli in person.
shelter_address: 1000 Cobb Place Blvd NW, Suite 450, Kennesaw, GA 30144
scoring_notes:
• Shy disposition requires patient adopter, narrowing the pool but appealing to readers who love a slow-burn bond
• Backstory is thin — came from animal control recently, no distinctive arc to drive newsletter engagement
• Recently arrived with siblings still present, suggesting a short shelter stay with low urgency

blurb:
Souffle came into this world in someone's garage, born to a feral mom who couldn't stick around. A good neighbor helped get her and her siblings to Mostly Mutts, and most of those siblings have already found homes. Souffle got a little held up along the way because she came down with an upper respiratory infection. Sniffly, tired, not quite herself. But she's on the other side of it now, and the volunteers say her energy is coming back fast.

She's a tuxedo kitten, about 2.5 months old and roughly 2 pounds. Sweet and affectionate. She likes chasing toys, pouncing on shadows, and curling up close to the people taking care of her. While she was sick, she leaned hard into the cuddle time. Now that she's feeling better, she's starting to add some chaos back into the mix.

Souffle does great with other kittens and would probably love having a feline friend at home. Her history with dogs is unknown, so a meet and greet would be a good idea if you have one. She's litter trained, up to date on shots, and microchipped. Adoption fee is $175.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. You can browse their available cats and reach out through the link below to set up a visit.
shelter_address: 1000 Cobb Place Blvd NW, Suite 450, Kennesaw, GA 30144
scoring_notes:
• Tuxedo kitten with classic crowd-pleasing appeal — affectionate, playful, and fully recovered from illness
• Underdog arc (sick kitten bouncing back) gives readers an easy emotional hook
• Siblings already adopted signals she's been waiting longer than the rest of the litter

blurb:
Someone found Strawberry and her siblings outside without their mom when they were tiny. A good citizen scooped them up, got them to Mostly Mutts, and from there they bounced through a couple of foster homes while they grew big enough to be adopted. Strawberry is now living in the Kitty Room at the shelter, and she's ready.

She's a tuxedo kitten, about 2.5 months old and roughly 2.5 pounds. Volunteers describe her as sweet, playful, and affectionate, which honestly tracks for a kitten who spent her early weeks getting a lot of hands-on attention in foster care. She loves wand toys, crinkly balls, and running through the cat tunnel with the other kittens. When it's time to wind down, she's equally happy in a lap or piled up with her siblings.

She gets along great with other cats and would likely do well with gentle kids. Dog history is unknown. She's litter trained, up to date on shots, and microchipped. Adoption fee is $175.

Mostly Mutts is located in Kennesaw. Head to their website to learn more or stop by to meet her.
shelter_address: 1000 Cobb Place Blvd NW, Suite 450, Kennesaw, GA 30144
scoring_notes:
• Highly adoptable profile — socialized through multiple foster homes, great with kids and cats, zero red flags
• Foster-to-shelter transition adds a layered journey that readers can follow and root for
• Multiple foster placements before landing in the shelter room suggests a longer-than-average road to adoption
